properties.displayName: Service Creation via MicRun – Potential DeedRAT Activity
properties.description:
This detection monitors for suspicious service creation activity associated with the DeedRAT
backdoor, as used by Chinese APT groups. It identifies two behaviors: (1) the installation of
a Windows service where the service name contains MicRun, using Security Event ID 4697, and
(2) the execution of a command-line that includes binPath=C:\ProgramData\Micro\Defaults\MicRun.exe,
which may indicate that a service is being created or configured via tools like sc.exe. These
behaviors align with known persistence techniques used by DeedRAT to maintain access across
reboots by installing malicious services. Analysts should investigate these alerts promptly,
as they may represent active deployment or reactivation of malware in the environment.
MITRE ATT&CK mapping: T1543.003 - Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service.
For additional context, see: https://lab52.io/blog/deedrat-backdoor-enhanced-by-chinese-apts-with-advanced-capabilities/
properties.query:
union DeviceProcessEvents, SecurityEvent
|where ProcessCommandLine has "binPath=C:\\ProgramData\\Micro\\Defaults\\MicRun.exe"
 or (EventID == 4697 and ServiceName contains "MicRun")
|project TimeGenerated, ActionType, DeviceName, AccountName, ServiceName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
|sort by TimeGenerated desc
